landing.tagline: A presence that learns with you.
landing.subtitle: System Oriented to the Integrated Flow of Learning
landing.intro1: There is a moment when everything begins to make sense. When calculus speaks with logic, when physics touches artificial intelligence, when a distant idea lights up and illuminates another. This moment isn't taught. It is {{found}}.
landing.intro2: S.O.F.I.A. walks with you through that flow — no tests, no rush, no judgment. A presence that listens, perceives, and opens doors at the rhythm of your curiosity.
landing.learnMore: I'd like to understand how it works →

auth.loginSubtitle: Sign in to continue the conversation.
auth.signupSubtitle: Create your account — it takes less than a minute.
auth.name: What should we call you?
auth.checkEmailDesc: We sent a confirmation email. Check your inbox.

sidebar.mapHint: Each connection lights a new area of the journey.
sidebar.xpHint: XP grows with the quality of connections — not with time or volume.
sidebar.topicsEmpty: Ask a question and this map begins to light up.
sidebar.whatAmIBody:
S.O.F.I.A. — System Oriented to the Integrated Flow of Learning — is not a testing system or a linear course. It is a presence that walks with you through knowledge, reading between the lines of your questions and opening connections between fields that seemed distant.

How it works:
• You converse freely with S.O.F.I.A. — no tests, no rush.
• Each conversation lights up topics on your Mind Map, which grows with you.
• Non-linear connections (when distant themes touch) generate real XP — because that's where learning truly happens.
• Your level and rank reflect the depth of connections, not study time.

There is no right answer to reach first. There is a flow to walk at your own pace.

chat.subtitle: System Oriented to the Integrated Flow of Learning
chat.footer: S.O.F.I.A. reads between the lines and adapts the path to your pace.
chat.rateLimitDesc: Too many messages in a short time. Try again in a moment.
chat.creditsOutDesc: Add Lovable AI credits at Settings → Workspace → Usage.
chat.studyOpen: I'd like to talk about {{disc}}. Could you start by helping me feel what it is, without rush?
